content: We can frame the original Self-Refine framework for iterative output improvement as a single-perspective baseline that can be extended by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refinement framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra supervised training or reinforcement learning. This hybrid framework would resolve the key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine's tendency to get stuck in local iterative loops of unrecognized flaws by leveraging contrasting perspectives to surface blind spots the original single feedback pass misses.
